message:
fix(patterns): bound retry-phrase→duration gap so DOTALL doesn't re-open false positive (ft-y820u follow-on)

b8468df5's `(?s)` flag fixed the multi-line false-negative but
silently re-opened the ft-fdppm false-positive: with `.` matching
`\n`, the trailing `.*?(?P<retry_after>\d+...)` happily pulled the
distant unrelated digits across the 800-char gap in the regression
fixture. The 200-char `.{0,200}?` proximity bound only constrained
anchor→retry-phrase, not retry-phrase→duration; pre-(?s), the
implicit newline-non-matching of `.*?` enforced the second gap by
accident.

Tighten the second gap with an explicit `.{0,80}?` between the
retry phrase and the duration capture. 80 chars accommodates every
realistic shape ("retry in 30 seconds" = 4 chars, "back off for 5
minutes" = 13 chars) but rejects the 800-char regression fixture.

Verified via cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib rate_limit_rules:
  - rate_limit_rules_match_across_newlines_within_proximity_bound  ok
  - rate_limit_rules_require_anchor_proximity_to_retry_duration    ok
  2/2 pass in 1m07s on /tmp/ft-swarm-violetmoth-target.

This restores the ft-fdppm false-positive guard that b8468df5
inadvertently broke. Both the multi-line positive (ft-y820u) and
the 800-char-gap negative (ft-fdppm) now hold simultaneously.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
